message:
chore(beads): close bd-sttk9 fuzz_read_write harness

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-sh2ms","title":"syscall: pidfd_getfd self pidfd with invalid flags should return EINVAL exactly","description":"Host glibc returns EINVAL for pidfd_getfd(self_pidfd, 1, 1). Add focused ABI regression coverage for the invalid-flags contract on a valid pidfd.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T09:52:52.477053379Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-22T04:54:24.500827639Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T04:54:24.498299060Z","close_reason":"pidfd_getfd(self, 0, 1) → EINVAL (non-zero flags reserved) pinned.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-sq73","title":"syscall: eventfd_write valid fd forbidden all-ones should return EINVAL exactly","description":"Host glibc returns EINVAL for eventfd_write(valid_fd, UINT64_MAX). Add focused ABI regression coverage so the forbidden all-ones payload contract is frozen exactly.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:37:58.401201421Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-22T00:49:57.440400208Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T00:49:57.438116866Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-srp3","title":"Conformance: direct execute locale_ops fixture cases","description":"Add direct execute_fixture_case coverage for locale_ops alongside existing isolated harness subprocess coverage so locale fixtures are checked against the shared executor API directly.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SilverHarbor","created_at":"2026-04-18T12:21:08.411431385Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T12:27:12.236891412Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T12:27:12.234942152Z","close_reason":"Added direct execute_fixture_case coverage for locale_ops fixtures alongside existing isolated harness mode-contract coverage; verified with rch focused test, check, and clippy.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","locale","testing"]}
-{"id":"bd-sttk9","title":"fuzz: read/write/pread/pwrite buffer + count validation","description":"## Scope
Add crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_read_write.rs targeting frankenlibc_abi::unistd_abi::{read,write,pread,pwrite,readv,writev}. Focus on buffer-bound validation + short-read/short-write semantics + errno correctness for invalid fd/len/buf.

## Archetype
1 + 3: Crash-detector + **differential** against libc for matched calls, using a seeded pipe pair and /dev/zero / /dev/null fds as deterministic backends.

## Inputs
struct ReadWriteFuzzInput {
  op: u8,                 // 0=read, 1=write, 2=pread, 3=pwrite, 4=readv, 5=writev
  fd_selector: u8,        // 0=/dev/zero, 1=/dev/null, 2=pipe-read-end, 3=pipe-write-end, 4=closed-fd, 5=-1
  buf_len: u16,           // 0..=32768
  buf_offset: u8,         // shift within a larger allocation (exercises decide's address checks)
  poison_ptr: bool,       // NULL or address we can catch
  offset: i64,            // for pread/pwrite
  iovec_count: u8,        // 0..=8 for readv/writev
}

## Invariants
- No panic on any input
- rc is a non-negative byte count OR -1 with errno in {EBADF, EFAULT, EINVAL, EIO, EAGAIN, EINTR, EPIPE, ENOSPC, ESPIPE, EOVERFLOW}
- Host parity for the /dev/zero, /dev/null, closed-fd cases: ours matches libc::read/write bit-for-bit
- readv/writev total byte count equals the sum of per-iovec byte counts
- Buffer is not read/written past requested len (ASAN + explicit sentinel check before and after)

## Expected bug classes
- Off-by-one in iovec loop, iov_len overflow, fd validation bypass, membrane decision mismatches for cross-page buffers, missing EINTR retry, sign handling for large counts (SSIZE_MAX), ESPIPE not mapped for pread/pwrite on pipes

## Sentinel pattern
Allocate 2*buf_len + 2 pages, place guard bytes (0xAA) before and after the requested window; after the call assert guard bytes unchanged.

## Score
5 × 5 × /2 ≈ 12.5 — tied with bd-o4yda for priority.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:39:39.154205881Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T09:01:44.053043033Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-sttk9","depends_on_id":"bd-jy4qu","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:01:44.052743346Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Add crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_read_write.rs targeting frankenlibc_abi::unistd_abi::{read,write,pread,pwrite,readv,writev}. Focus on buffer-bound validation + short-read/short-write semantics + errno correctness for invalid fd/len/buf.

## Archetype
1 + 3: Crash-detector + **differential** against libc for matched calls, using a seeded pipe pair and /dev/zero / /dev/null fds as deterministic backends.

## Inputs
struct ReadWriteFuzzInput {
  op: u8,                 // 0=read, 1=write, 2=pread, 3=pwrite, 4=readv, 5=writev
  fd_selector: u8,        // 0=/dev/zero, 1=/dev/null, 2=pipe-read-end, 3=pipe-write-end, 4=closed-fd, 5=-1
  buf_len: u16,           // 0..=32768
  buf_offset: u8,         // shift within a larger allocation (exercises decide's address checks)
  poison_ptr: bool,       // NULL or address we can catch
  offset: i64,            // for pread/pwrite
  iovec_count: u8,        // 0..=8 for readv/writev
}

## Invariants
- No panic on any input
- rc is a non-negative byte count OR -1 with errno in {EBADF, EFAULT, EINVAL, EIO, EAGAIN, EINTR, EPIPE, ENOSPC, ESPIPE, EOVERFLOW}
- Host parity for the /dev/zero, /dev/null, closed-fd cases: ours matches libc::read/write bit-for-bit
- readv/writev total byte count equals the sum of per-iovec byte counts
- Buffer is not read/written past requested len (ASAN + explicit sentinel check before and after)

Allocate 2*buf_len + 2 pages, place guard bytes (0xAA) before and after the requested window; after the call assert guard bytes unchanged.

## Score
5 × 5 × /2 ≈ 12.5 — tied with bd-o4yda for priority.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:39:39.154205881Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T09:21:22.578845312Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T09:21:22.576538966Z","close_reason":"fuzz_read_write harness committed and green: 16.3M execs / 108K exec/s in 151s. Covers read, write, pread, pwrite, readv, writev against 5 deterministic fd backends (/dev/zero, /dev/null, pipe read, pipe write, closed fd, wild fd). Sentinel-byte guards before/after every buffer window catch any over-count write. Differential parity against libc for non-pipe backends. Two harness-side bugs fixed during bring-up (count > buf_size overrun, buf_offset into post-pad). Zero real impl bugs under the invariant set — the read/write wrapper is solid. Committed 4e263808.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-sttk9","depends_on_id":"bd-jy4qu","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:01:44.052743346Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-t1je","title":"syscall: sched_getaffinity invalid pid with null mask should preserve ESRCH exactly","description":"Host glibc returns ESRCH for sched_getaffinity(-1, sizeof(cpu_set_t), NULL), so the invalid-pid contract dominates the null mask pointer. Add ABI regression coverage for that precedence.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T09:14:15.079730369Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-22T03:20:17.207935381Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T03:20:17.205591083Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Verify with focused rch test/check/clippy.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SilverHarbor","created_at":"2026-04-18T03:08:38.497150192Z","created_by":"SilverHarbor","updated_at":"2026-04-18T03:12:53.515486157Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T03:12:53.511957811Z","close_reason":"Implemented executable ctype_ops fixture coverage; verified with rch test/check/clippy","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","ctype","testing"]}
